world_date: Month of the Pale Reckoning, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord
major_event: Arkenor scouts and Elowen's war-bands make first contact on the Frostvale ridge. No blades are drawn — but an Arkenor lieutenant is taken alive, and what he says under Elven questioning changes Elowen's face entirely. She dismisses all witnesses before he finishes speaking.

recent_events.text: Arkenor and Elven forces met at the Pale Ridge narrows. The Arkenor lieutenant surrendered voluntarily — no weapon raised. Elowen heard his message privately. The war-bands have been ordered to hold position and say nothing.

A sound rose from the open gate at midday — not a voice, but a rhythm. Hammering. Deliberate, patient, growing louder. Kessa Stonebraid walked toward it and had to be physically restrained by two soldiers. She did not resist. She was smiling.

Roderic emerged from his library on the fourth day. He summoned not his court but a single name: Ser Aldric Thorne. The audience was private. Aldric has not been seen publicly since entering the king's chamber.

Rowen finally ate. He called his three senior captains, spread the charts, and spoke for two hours. When they emerged, all three had the look of men who had been told they were already dead. Rowen had made his decision.

The Druidic Conclave convened in full for the first time in eleven years. The Grand Druid opened the session by unsealing a chamber that had been locked since before most members were born. What was inside has not been described.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld Dwarves — Deep Entity vs All Surface Factions
active_tensions.description: The hammering from below has begun. Kessa's compulsion toward the gate is escalating. Ironmaul's missives have been received but responses have not arrived. The mountain is running out of time.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court vs Glenhaven Elves — Frostvale Ridge Contact
active_tensions.description: The Arkenor lieutenant's voluntary surrender and private message to Elowen suggests Lythara sent him — not as a scout but as a courier. The war may not be what either side believed it was.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Roderic vs Aldric vs Tidefall
active_tensions.description: Roderic's private summons of Aldric is unprecedented and alarming to the court. If the two brothers have reached terms, it either stabilizes the succession or weaponizes it against Tidefall.

active_tensions.factions: Dreadwind Islands vs Tidefall — Submerged Structures
active_tensions.description: Rowen has shared the charts with his captains and made a decision. The sea routes around the Tidefall outer lanes are about to become contested in a way no naval doctrine has prepared for.

character_updates.name: Kessa Stonebraid
character_updates.status: Attempted to walk into the open gate when the hammering began. Restrained. She has since been confined to quarters but the symbols on her neck now emit faint light — visible through cloth.

character_updates.name: Thane Ulric Ironmaul
character_updates.status: Has received no responses to his missives yet. He has begun sleeping in his chair outside Kessa's confinement room. He has stopped eating regularly. He is waiting for something to arrive before it is too late.

character_updates.name: Sovereign Elowen Silverleaf
character_updates.status: Dismissed all witnesses from the Arkenor lieutenant's message. Has sent new orders to the ridge war-bands — still no engagement — but added a second instruction no courier has been willing to repeat aloud.

character_updates.name: Prince Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Received Elowen's spoken-word rider. Has gone very still since. He accepted the Frostvale council audience, but spoke very little. He is now corresponding with someone via the Gnome pass routes.

character_updates.name: King Roderic Thorne II
character_updates.status: Summoned Aldric to private audience. Neither has emerged in two days. The court assumes negotiation. The royal steward has begun quietly counting the guard rotations outside the chamber door.

character_updates.name: Pirate King Rowen
character_updates.status: Decision made. Three captains briefed on both submerged structures. Fleet is being repositioned — not toward Tidefall, but toward the sinking island. Rowen has sent one message, recipient unknown.

faction_morale.reason: The hammering from below has broken the last of the garrison's composure. Seven more have deserted. Kessa's confinement and glowing symbols are an open secret. The mountain feels like a tomb being prepared.
Lythara's use of the Arkenor lieutenant as courier rather than combatant suggests a pivot in strategy. Velorn and Nythariel remain excluded and are beginning to resent it openly.
The ridge war-bands read the Arkenor surrender as Elven dominance. Elowen has not corrected this interpretation. Morale is high on a foundation she knows to be complicated.
Roderic and Aldric's sealed audience has paralyzed court function. No edicts, no appointments, no responses to Tidefall's counter-offer. The thirty-day clock ticks in a political vacuum.
The Admiral reads Roderic's silence as capitulation-in-progress. His flagship sits in harbor like a throne. He has begun entertaining other envoys aboard it — Dur Khadur among them.

The unsealed chamber has renewed the Conclave's sense of purpose and dread in equal measure. The Grand Druid moves with the certainty of a man who now knows what he is fighting, even if others do not.
Rowen's decision and the fleet's repositioning have restored his captains' faith. They do not fully understand the plan, but kings who move with purpose inspire more than kings who sit with charts.
Both pass-crossing teams have reached their destinations. What they carried or retrieved has not been disclosed. The Syndicate has opened a third route — one not on any known map.

whispers.text: The message the Arkenor lieutenant carried to Elowen was from Lythara herself — a warning, not a threat. Something beneath Lostfeld concerns the Shadow Court as much as anyone above ground.
The unsealed Druidic chamber contained a body. It was seated upright, preserved without decay, wearing robes from an era before the Hollow Accord. Its hands were folded over a closed book.
Rowen's unknown message recipient is on the sinking island. He has been before — once, years ago — under a name no one in the Dreadwind fleet knows him by.
Caeris's correspondence through the Gnome pass routes is addressed to neither Elowen nor Aldric. The Syndicate knows who receives it and has chosen, so far, not to sell that knowledge.

weather_and_omens.condition: The hammering from below carries a rhythm scholars of the Conclave recognize — it matches the ceremonial pulse used in pre-Accord binding rites. It is counting down to something.
The upward-falling snow now forms the same unidentified letters each time, in the same sequence. A Gnome cartographer passing through has begun writing them down without explaining why.
The luminescence in Eldoria harbor has spread beyond the Tidefall hulls — it now traces a shape across the harbor floor visible from the docks. It matches no known chart of the seabed.
The moon-shadow over the eastern ridge has begun to shrink. Whatever it was marking, the marking is done. The forest beneath it has gone completely silent — no birds, no insects, nothing.

Three whaling vessels reported the same vision independently: a tower rising from the water for a moment at dusk, then sinking. All three ships changed course away from the Tidefall lanes without being ordered to.

absorbed_lore: The Arkenor lieutenant surrendered voluntarily on the Frostvale ridge and delivered a private message to Elowen — almost certainly from Lythara herself, possibly a warning about the Deep Entity.
Elowen dismissed all witnesses before the lieutenant finished speaking and issued a second secret order to the ridge war-bands.
The hammering from below Lostfeld began at midday — rhythmic, deliberate, and matching pre-Accord binding rite cadence; it appears to be counting down.
Kessa Stonebraid had to be physically restrained from entering the open gate when the hammering started; she was smiling; her symbols now emit visible light through cloth.
Ironmaul is sleeping outside Kessa's confinement room and has stopped eating regularly; no missive responses have arrived yet.
Roderic summoned Aldric to a private audience; neither has emerged in two days; the court steward is counting guard rotations.
Rowen made his decision: fleet repositioned not toward Tidefall but toward the sinking island; he has sent one message to an unknown recipient who may be on the sinking island.
Rowen visited the sinking island once before under an unknown name — this is known to almost no one.
The Druidic Conclave unsealed a chamber locked for generations; inside was a seated, preserved body in pre-Accord robes, hands folded over a closed book.
The Grand Druid now moves with the certainty of a man who knows what he is fighting.
Gloomspire Gnomes' two pass-crossing teams reached their destinations; a third previously unmapped route has been opened.
Caeris is corresponding via Gnome pass routes with someone who is neither Elowen nor Aldric; the Syndicate knows and has not yet sold the information.
The Tidefall Admiral has begun entertaining Dur Khadur envoys aboard his flagship — political overreach while the Twin Cities court is paralyzed.
Three whaling vessels independently reported a tower rising briefly from the sea near the Tidefall lanes at dusk before sinking again.
The luminescent shape in Eldoria harbor now traces a pattern across the seabed matching no known chart.
